summary.name: IvySets Stock Factor Lab
summary.model: One-week statistical center and 68% range from 20-day drift and realized volatility; not a guaranteed price target.

stocks.next_step: Research fundamentals and wait for a defined entry setup; the model range is statistical, not a target price.

factors.label: Current price
factors.definition: Latest adjusted market price from daily data.

factors.label: Market cap
factors.definition: Price multiplied by shares outstanding where SEC shares are available.

factors.label: Enterprise value
factors.definition: Market cap plus debt minus cash.

factors.label: Distance from 52W high
factors.definition: Current price relative to one-year high.

factors.label: Distance from 52W low
factors.definition: Current price relative to one-year low.

factors.label: 20D realized volatility
factors.definition: Annualized realized volatility from 20 daily log returns.

factors.label: 60D realized volatility
factors.definition: Annualized realized volatility from 60 daily log returns.

factors.label: 1Y beta vs SPY
factors.definition: Covariance with SPY divided by SPY variance over one year.

factors.label: 1Y correlation vs SPY
factors.definition: Correlation of daily returns with SPY.

factors.label: 1Y max drawdown
factors.definition: Largest peak-to-trough loss over one year.

factors.label: 60D downside volatility
factors.definition: Annualized volatility of negative daily returns.

factors.label: ATR 14 as % price
factors.definition: Average true range over 14 days divided by price.

factors.label: 1W statistical center
factors.definition: Current price projected by 20D average daily log return over five days.

factors.label: 1W 68% low
factors.definition: Center minus one recent-volatility standard deviation over five days.

factors.label: 1W 68% high
factors.definition: Center plus one recent-volatility standard deviation over five days.

factors.label: Revenue
factors.definition: Latest annual revenue from SEC companyfacts.

factors.label: Revenue growth YoY
factors.definition: Latest annual revenue growth versus prior year.

factors.label: Diluted EPS
factors.definition: Latest annual diluted earnings per share.

factors.label: EPS growth YoY
factors.definition: Latest annual diluted EPS growth versus prior year.

factors.label: Free cash flow
factors.definition: Operating cash flow minus capex cash outflow.

factors.label: FCF margin
factors.definition: Free cash flow divided by revenue.

factors.label: CFO / net income
factors.definition: Operating cash flow divided by net income.

factors.label: Total debt
factors.definition: Short-term plus long-term debt when available.

factors.label: Debt / equity
factors.definition: Total debt divided by shareholders equity.

factors.label: P/E
factors.definition: Market price divided by diluted EPS.

factors.label: P/FCF
factors.definition: Market cap divided by free cash flow.

factors.label: EV/EBITDA
factors.definition: Enterprise value divided by estimated EBITDA.

factors.label: R&D / sales
factors.definition: Research and development divided by revenue.

factor_catalog.label: Debt / equity
factor_catalog.definition: Total debt divided by shareholders equity.

factor_catalog.label: P/E
factor_catalog.definition: Market price divided by diluted EPS.

factor_catalog.label: P/FCF
factor_catalog.definition: Market cap divided by free cash flow.

factor_catalog.label: EV/EBITDA
factor_catalog.definition: Enterprise value divided by estimated EBITDA.

factor_catalog.label: R&D / sales
factor_catalog.definition: Research and development divided by revenue.

literature.study: Fama and French (1992, 1993): size and book-to-market help explain the cross-section of average stock returns.
literature.dashboard_use: P/E, P/B, P/S, P/FCF and market-cap context are used as valuation and size descriptors, not standalone predictions.

literature.study: Fama and French (2015): the five-factor model adds profitability and investment to market, size and value factors.
literature.dashboard_use: Margins, ROA, ROE, FCF margin and asset growth are treated as quality/investment factors.

literature.study: Jegadeesh and Titman (1993): stocks with strong intermediate-term returns historically continued to outperform over subsequent horizons.
literature.dashboard_use: 20D, 63D, 126D and 252D returns plus relative strength versus SPY are used for momentum ranking.

literature.study: Carhart (1997): adds a momentum factor to explain persistence in mutual fund performance.
literature.dashboard_use: Momentum is separated from fundamental quality rather than mixed into valuation.

literature.study: Piotroski (2000): accounting-based signals can separate stronger from weaker value stocks.
literature.dashboard_use: ROA, operating cash flow, accrual-style CFO/net income, leverage and margin factors are shown explicitly.

literature.study: Sloan (1996): earnings components related to accruals have different persistence and implications for future returns.
literature.dashboard_use: Operating cash flow versus net income and FCF margin are included as earnings-quality checks.

literature.study: Novy-Marx (2013): gross profitability contains information related to expected returns.
literature.dashboard_use: Gross margin and gross profit are included in the quality score.

literature.study: Campbell, Hilscher and Szilagyi (2008): financial distress risk is related to future equity returns and poor firm fundamentals.
literature.dashboard_use: Debt/equity, liabilities/assets, cash/assets and interest coverage are used as balance-sheet risk descriptors.

literature.study: Ang, Hodrick, Xing and Zhang (2006): idiosyncratic volatility is related to cross-sectional returns, with important risk implications.
literature.dashboard_use: 20D/60D volatility, beta, correlation, downside deviation, ATR and drawdown are used for risk ranking and forecast range width.

literature.study: Market-efficiency/random-walk literature implies exact short-horizon prices are not scientifically knowable from public data alone.
literature.dashboard_use: The website shows a one-week statistical center and 68% range from recent drift and volatility, not a guaranteed target price.
